blockers.why: The baseline emitted provisional identifiers. Public source records now expose syntactically current candidates, but no verifiable owner-signed re-anchor records, out-of-band estate-owner anchor, or authenticated section 13 registry was provided.
blockers.required_action: The estate owner must explicitly retire each historical claim or provide the complete authorization and registry evidence required by RAPP/1 sections 6.3, 10, and 13. Repository maintainers must not infer, mint, re-anchor, or sign on the owner's behalf.

acceptance_tests.assertion: Every accepted identity matches section 6.1, has section 6.2 mint provenance, and reuses its stored tail unless a lawful re-anchor is owner-authorized.
Every accepted migration has an owner-signed section 13.3 re-anchor record and the continuity, tombstone, or upgrade evidence required by section 6.3.
The section 13 registry signature verifies from the out-of-band estate-owner anchor, its sequence is monotonic, and its source and freshness satisfy the owner-defined policy.
Only owner-accepted public identities are emitted; private or unresolved locators, provisional identifiers, and unauthenticated candidates remain absent.
Any future public inventory states its non-protocol schema and semantics without redefining RAPP/1, and any Router/Mirror remains subordinate to kody-w/RAPP.

prohibited_substitutes: A moving branch URL without a resolved commit and byte hash
A non-authoritative observation manifest presented as owner authorization
A syntactically valid identifier without mint and authorization provenance
A self-asserted migration field without an owner-signed re-anchor record
An unsigned or stale registry candidate
A locally invented key, signature, tail, re-anchor, tombstone, owner decision, or acceptance state
